title: Chapter Twelve: Public Duel
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"A white robe challenged a blue robe?"

"To be precise, they are calling it a sparring match for guidance."

"That is not it. I know the inside story."

The news spread through every corner of the Desolate Sect. A new recruit and a blue-rank warrior of the Azure Sky Squad were going to cross swords for the honour of their squads.

The consensus was that the gap between white and blue was wider than it appeared. No one was interested in the outcome.

"How many seconds will he last?"

"Ten at most."

"A white robe lasting more than ten moves against a blue robe? That picture cannot be drawn."

On the day of the duel, even warriors from other squads and the General Administration had come to watch. The Azure Sky Squad training ground was packed. Hundreds of faces, blue robes and white, a few scattered black -- supreme masters come to judge. The air hummed with the accumulated aura of the Desolate Sect's finest.

Swords. Only swords. Every weapon rack held them, dozens of blades in every size and shape -- thin rapiers, broad dao, curved sabres and straight jian polished to mirror brightness. The Azure Sky Squad's identity, forged in steel.

*An entire squad that trains exclusively in the sword until they reach blue rank. The depth of that dedication is terrifying.*

Yeon-shin surveyed the crowd. The Azure Sky Squad warriors wore displeasure openly. A white-robe rookie requesting a duel was an insult, regardless of the diplomatic language. Some of the younger ones muttered behind their hands, their eyes hard.

Behind him, ten Radiant Demon Wing children had pressed themselves against the barrier. They had followed on foot, all the way from the Radiant Demon compound, and their small faces were fierce with loyalty.

"You have to last at least five moves!" one of them shouted. "We made a bet with the Azure Sky kids! Brother! Over here!"

"You cannot tarnish the name of Radiant Demon!"

"Master! Fighting!"

*Master, is it.*

Yeon-shin chuckled. The children sometimes called him and Heon Won-chang master as a joke, but to a boy who had never been able to form attachments to a family, the word was fresh and sweet. He waved to them. A cheer erupted. Pure, bright-eyed children.

On one side, two supreme masters in black robes stared at each other. Ma Jin and the Azure Sky Squad Leader -- a tall, lean swordsman famous as one of the Ten Great Swordsmen of Huguang. Long arms, powerful eyes.

"Let us bet one of the squad's spirit pills on whether he lasts ten seconds," the Squad Leader said. "I will bet he does not."

"Spirit pills. Is that not excessive?"

"Are you scared?"

"What childish provocation. The spirit pills of the Desolate Sect are no light matter."

"And the honour of our squads is?"

Ma Jin's face was resignation itself. But the Squad Leader pressed harder, sensing blood. "You seem unprecedentedly weak, Ma Jin. Did you make such a fuss to bring in a boy like this?"

"Fine. I accept."

"Good. A leader should trust his subordinates."

The Squad Leader turned away. He did not see Ma Jin smile.

---

"You said your name is Jeong Yeon-shin."

"Radiant Demon Wing. Jeong Yeon-shin."

He saluted with a fist-and-palm gesture. The words felt right in a way they never had before. Not *Jeong Yeon-shin of Xinye*. Not the third son of a dead concubine. Not the boy who killed his mother in the birthing.

Radiant Demon Wing. The name that modified his current self. From the Blood Massacre of the Jeong Family to Nanyang in Henan and Xiangyang in Huguang -- this was where the road had led, and this was who he had become. Regardless of how others viewed it, the duel representing an entire squad felt unfamiliar and precious.

The warrior stood ten paces ahead. Blue robes. Fierce eyes. A spirit that rose off him like heat from a forge.

"I have trained in the Azure Sky Sword Technique for twenty years," the warrior said. "A sword has no eyes, but the sword of a master who has reached the pinnacle does. I do not leave leeway in my hands. Remember that."

"Aye."

The warrior's eyebrow twitched. He placed his hand on his hilt. A frosty aura stretched outward.

*What kind of sword?*

Yeon-shin was already reading him through the Radiant Demon Formation. True energy extending outward, testing, mapping. The Azure Sky Sword Technique was said to have many faces -- swift sword, heavy sword, depending on the practitioner.

*Blue-rank warriors master all of them.*

He stirred his own energy. The Fate Conquering Scripture awakened, true qi overlapping through his open meridians. Two stages of efficiency. Twice the power of ordinary circulation.

"Come," the warrior said. He yielded the first strike.

Yeon-shin breathed in.

The moment the breath caught in his chest, he drew.

True energy stamped the ground. The formula of the swift sword lived on the blade of the Desolate Sword, distorting the sunlight. Force rose from beneath his feet, condensed, and the sword traced the path that Radiant Demon Formation had drawn.

The blade screamed.

He smiled at the vibration that sang up through his grip.

*It went in.*

The warrior's nonchalance shattered. His centre of gravity broke. His balance shifted, and in that shift Yeon-shin saw everything.

He pushed off the ground with the same foot. Closed the distance. The warrior's body grew larger in his vision, the scenery a blur at the edges.

*Now. If not now, I cannot win.*

Steel met steel.

The resonance was tremendous. His sword force had grown in ways he had not anticipated. The warrior's eyes went serious, but the surging blade denied him a proper stance. Each cut flowed from the last -- white streaks filling the air between them.

Ten exchanges. Fifteen. Twenty.

Yeon-shin forgot to count. He was immersed in a state of selflessness, comprehending the Radiant Demon Formation with every fibre of his being.

The principles accumulated. When the sword path tilted, he used his back muscles. When he stepped diagonally, the twist of his foot generated rotational force. True energy spiralled along the rotation.

*Cheong Myeong's rotational strike. I understand it now.*

He swung. The whirling true energy was contained in the blow.

The warrior retreated a step.

Yeon-shin raised his sword. Rotated.

A powerful wind scattered in all directions -- a fragment of explosive force, the power of meridians unleashed.

*Charged Rotational Strike.*

The name came as naturally as breathing. And with it, the understanding that the force could be chained. One rotation feeding the next. Each blow stronger than the last.

He spun again. The second strike followed the first.

The warrior's Desolate Sword went flying. His arm was thrown above his head, his body shaken to the roots. He staggered, nearly falling.

A sword touched beneath his chin.

Yeon-shin held it there. His expression was strange -- part wonder, part disbelief.

*It was luck. A timely flash of inspiration added to surging momentum. If we fought again, the result would be different.*

The warrior's blue robe was remarkably clean. Only a few wrinkles. But the white robe had won.

Silence in the training ground.

Then the voices came.

"How many seconds was that?"

"They exchanged at least twenty moves."

"Exchanged? No. He won. Jeong Yeon-shin -- an astonishing age, and even more astonishing prowess."

"Radiant Demon Wing brought in a treasure."

Yeon-shin sheathed his sword. He clasped his fists in salute -- sincerely, from the belly. Gratitude. For the Charged Rotational Strike. For the goal the warrior's skill had set before him.

"Thank you for the opportunity to witness such exceptional swordsmanship."

"Your sword path and rotational strike were remarkable. Prowess that transcends age."

The warrior's mouth twitched upward. He rose to his feet. The pride was wounded, aye, but the respect was real.

Yeon-shin turned. Found the children of Radiant Demon Wing staring with their mouths open.

He squatted down to their eye level.

"Radiant Demon Wing. This much should not tarnish our name, aye? Enjoy the candy."

Their stunned faces came alive. Eyes sparkling. One of them -- the smallest, a girl with cropped hair and a wooden practice sword strapped to her back -- whispered *Master* in a voice thick with awe. Yeon-shin felt the crown of his head pulse. Not pain. Not the burning. Something warmer.

The dumbfounded faces of the Radiant Demon Wing children were amusing. They had bet on five moves. Their brother had won outright.

Behind them, the Azure Sky Squad Leader was biting his lower lip. Yeon-shin saluted him with the same sincerity he had given the warrior, and the Squad Leader's jaw tightened -- fury and respect warring on his angular face.

Through the crowd, Ma Jin strode toward him. A fragrant scent emanated from his outstretched palm. On it sat a round lump of medicine, pulsing with concentrated energy.

A spirit pill.

"It is yours," Ma Jin said, pulling up the scar below his lip.

---
